text: Release-hardening pass on the openreview repo ahead of tagging its first public release, driven by the user repeatedly walking the "happy path" and reporting friction. Work centered on the dashboard/server UX and the README: reframing the README around steady-state operation (dashboard open in a browser while Claude runs a study) with added visuals and clearer explanation that the whole repo lives "in the data," plus guidance in skills so users know to ask Claude to start a study while the dashboard runs. Added user alerts for when the card first appears and when the eval is ready, handling for a study being deleted out from under an open dashboard, and cleaned up the top navigation into the four core areas (protocol, pipeline, findings, eval) while tidying the download/other clutter. The quality-gate eval section under findings was made collapsed-by-default (showing just pass, expanded on failure), the findings extraction table was made interactive so each row expands to full Record Explorer detail including links, and a chunk of the session dug into monitoring and communicating the browser-to-server connection state to the user. Ended with the user asking about the remaining weak link before posting release links on Reddit for Claude Code researchers.
